info.title: Voice API
info.x-metaTitle: Vonage Voice API Reference | Vonage API Documentation
info.description: The Voice API lets you create outbound calls, control in-progress calls and get information about historical calls. More information about the Voice API can be found at <https://developer.nexmo.com/voice/voice-api/overview>. To create IP calls please check the following page: <https://developer.vonage.com/en/api/voice.v2>. To create and manage calls within specific region, set the application region via [Application API](https://developer.vonage.com/en/api/application.v2) or use regional URLs as described in the [Regions](https://developer.vonage.com/en/voice/voice-api/concepts/regions) guide.
info.x-metaDescription: Our Voice API Reference provides developers with quick answers to questions about the Vonage Voice API. Learn more in Vonage's API documentation.

parameters.name: date_start
parameters.description: Return the records available after this point in time. Please use [Reports API](https://developer.vonage.com/en/reports/overview) for older call information.

parameters.name: date_end
parameters.description: Return the records that occurred before this point in time

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Return this amount of records in the response

parameters.name: record_index
parameters.description: Return calls from this index in the response

parameters.name: conversation_uuid
parameters.description: Return all the records associated with a specific conversation.

get.summary: Get detail of a specific call
get.description: Get detail of a specific call

put.summary: Play an audio file into a call
put.description: Play an audio file into a call

delete.summary: Stop playing an audio file into a call
delete.description: Stop playing an audio file into a call

put.summary: Play text to speech into a call
put.description: Play text to speech into a call

delete.summary: Stop text to speech in a call
delete.description: Stop text to speech in a call

put.summary: Play DTMF tones into a call
put.description: Play DTMF tones into a call

Register a listener to receive asynchronous DTMF inputs from a call. This is only applicable to [Input NCCO](https://developer.vonage.com/en/voice/voice-api/ncco-reference#input) events with the `mode` set to `asynchronous`. The payload delivered to this URL will be an [Input webhook event](https://localhost:3000/en/voice/voice-api/webhook-reference#input) with a single DTMF digit every time the callee enters DTMF into the call.

event_url.description: The URL (wrapped in an array) to send DTMF events to, as a POST request.

premium.description: Set to `true` to use the premium version of the specified style if available, otherwise the standard version will be used. You can find more information about Premium Voices in the [Text-To-Speech guide](https://developer.vonage.com/en/voice/voice-api/guides/text-to-speech#premium-voices).

loop.description: The number of times to repeat the text the file, 0 for infinite

level.description: The volume level that the speech is played. This can be any value between `-1` to `1` in `0.1` increments, with `0` being the default.

loop.description: the number of times to play the file, 0 for infinite

level.description: Set the audio level of the stream in the range `-1 >= level <= 1` with a precision of 0.1. The default value is 0.

RequestTransferActionParam.description: Transfer the call to a new NCCO

url.description: The URL that Vonage makes a request to. Must return an NCCO.

ncco.description: Refer to the [NCCO Guide](https://developer.nexmo.com/voice/voice-api/ncco-reference) for a description of possible NCCO parameters.

action.description: End the call for the specified UUID
Prevent the specified UUID from hearing audio
Allow the specified UUID to hear audio

ncco.description: The [Nexmo Call Control Object](/voice/voice-api/ncco-reference) to use for this call.

answer_url.description: The webhook endpoint where you provide the [Nexmo Call Control Object](/voice/voice-api/ncco-reference) that governs this call.

answer_method.description: The HTTP method used to send event information to answer_url.

random_from_number.description: Set to `true` to use random phone number as `from`. The number will be selected from the list of the numbers assigned to the current application. This property is mutually exclusive with `from`: you must specify one (`random_from_number: true`) or the other (`from`).

event_url.description:
**Required** unless `event_url` is configured at the application
level, see [Create an Application](/api/application.v2#createApplication)

The webhook endpoint where call progress events are
sent to. For more information about the values sent, see
[Event webhook](/voice/voice-api/webhook-reference#event-webhook).

event_method.description: The HTTP method used to send event information to `event_url`.

machine_detection.description: Configure the behavior when Vonage detects that the call is answered by voicemail. If `continue`, Vonage sends an HTTP request to `event_url` with the Call event machine. If `hangup`, Vonage ends the call.

advanced_machine_detection.description: Configure the behavior of Vonage's advanced machine detection. Overrides `machine_detection` if both are set. Note that this is a beta feature which will be chargeable; exact rates can be found on the [Voice API Pricing](https://www.vonage.co.uk/communications-apis/voice/pricing/) page under 'Programmable Features'.

behavior.description: When `hangup` is used, the call will be terminated if a machine is detected. When `continue` is used, the call will continue even if a machine is detected.

beep_timeout.description: Maximum time in seconds Vonage should wait for a machine beep to be detected. A machine event with `sub_state` set to `beep_timeout` will be sent if the timeout is exceeded.

length_timer.description: Set the number of seconds that elapse before Vonage hangs up after the call state changes to answered.

ringing_timer.description: Set the number of seconds that elapse before Vonage hangs up after the call state changes to ‘ringing’.

to.description: The single or mixed collection of endpoint types you connected to

count.title: The total number of records returned by your request.

page_size.title: The amount of records returned in this response.

_embedded.description: A list of call objects. See the [get details of a specific call](#getCall) response fields for a description of the nested objects

network.description: The Mobile Country Code Mobile Network Code ([MCCMNC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_country_code)) for the carrier network used to make this call. This is only sent if `status` is `completed`.
network.title: The Network ID of the destination

start_time.description: The time the call started in the following format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. For example, `2020-01-01 12:00:00`. This is only sent if `status` is `completed`.
start_time.title: The Start Time of the call

end_time.description: The time the call started in the following format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. For xample, `2020-01-01 12:00:00`. This is only sent if `status` is `completed`.
end_time.title: The End Time of the call

duration.description: The time elapsed for the call to take place in seconds. This is only sent if `status` is `completed`.
duration.title: The Duration of the call

price.description: The total price charged for this call. This is only sent if `status` is `completed`.
price.title: The total price of the call

rate.description: The price per minute for this call. This is only sent if `status` is `completed`.
rate.title: The Price per minute of the called destination

uuid.title: The UUID of the Call Leg
uuid.description: The unique identifier for this call leg. The UUID is created when your call request is accepted by Vonage. You use the UUID in all requests for individual live calls

conversation_uuid.title: The UUID of the Conversation
conversation_uuid.description: The unique identifier for the conversation this call leg is part of.

from.description: The endpoint you called from. Possible values are the same as `to`.

type.title: The type of Endpoint that made the call

number.title: The number that made the call

status.title: The State of the call
status.description: The status of the call. [See possible values](https://developer.nexmo.com/voice/voice-api/guides/call-flow#event-objects)

type.description: The type of connection. Must be `app`
The type of connection. Must be `phone`

dtmfAnswer.description: Provide [DTMF digits](https://developer.nexmo.com/voice/voice-api/concepts/dtmf) to send when the call is answered

shaken.description: For Vonage customers who are required by the FCC to sign their own calls to the USA, we allow you to place Voice API calls with your own signature. This feature is available by request only. Please be aware calls with an invalid signature will be rejected. For more information please contact us. In this option, you must place the STIR/SHAKEN Identity Header content that Vonage must use for this call. Expected format is composed of the JWT with the header, payload and signature, an info parameter with a link for the certificate, the algorithm (alg) parameter indicating which encryption type was used and the passport type (ppt) which should be shaken.

EndpointPhoneFrom.description: Connect to a Phone (PSTN) number. This property is mutually exclusive with `random_from_number`: you must specify one (`from`) or the other (`random_from_number: true`).

type.description: The type of connection. Must be `phone`.

number.description: The phone number to connect to. It can be either an E.164 formatted number or a SIP URI.

type.description: The type of connection. Must be `websocket`

headers.description: Details of the Websocket you want to connect to

customer_id.description: This is an example header. You can provide any headers you may need

type.description: The type of connection. Must be `sip`

headers.description: Metadata to include in the request. The headers are transmitted as part of the SIP INVITE as `X-key: value` headers.

standard_headers.description: Standard SIP INVITE headers. Unlike the `headers` property, these are not prepended with `X-`.

User-to-User.description: Transmit user-to-user information if supported by the CC / PBX vendor, as per [RFC 7433](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7433).

type.description: The type of connection. Must be `vbc`

AddressE164.description: The phone number to connect to

AddressSip.description: The SIP URI to connect to

style.description: The vocal style (vocal range, tessitura, and timbre) to use
